rationale: The citation is more than a passing mention: the paper explicitly uses Zhu and Dukes (2015) in its literature framing and discussion of media-bias mechanisms. It draws on that work to motivate the analogy that commercial intermediaries may strategically shape information and to reference the monopoly-versus-competition bias pattern. This helps position the new ranking model's contribution (a prestige-driven manipulation mechanism) relative to prior theory, even though the formal model is not directly reused.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Zhu and Dukes (2015) as prior conceptual groundwork on strategic media distortion and market-structure effects. It builds its positioning on that foundation by developing a related strategic-information model for university rankings with a distinct prestige channel.
